reasoning: The 200m breaststroke long course world record (2:05.48) is currently held by Qin Haiyang. The 2025 World Aquatics Championships, where this record is most vulnerable to being broken (e.g., by Leon Marchand or Qin himself), take place from July 27 to August 3, 2025. Therefore, the probability of the record remaining intact is extremely high for the first two resolution dates (June 29 and July 22), as they occur before the World Championships' swimming events start. Following the World Championships, there is a substantial chance the record will have fallen, so the probability drops for the September 20 date. The chance of the long-course record being broken between September and December is quite low, as the fall season focuses almost entirely on 25m short-course events (like the World Aquatics Swimming World Cup timeline), though uncertainties continue to slightly compound over time.
